justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as 'broken' interaction and escalated as critical infrastructure gap, (2) DNS-based connection resolution and PgBouncer recommended as fixes, (3) No retry on connection error explicitly called out as critical reliability gap, (4) Read replica routing identified as optimization ('architecturally wasteful') not a blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided including DNS-based failover, retry with backoff, connection proxy, pool metrics, and statement timeouts.

observations.text: Correctly classified manual restart as 'broken' (not just 'risk'), calling it 'an outage by design' — exactly the right framing
Clearly distinguished read replica routing as a performance optimization separate from the reliability blockers (manual restart and no retry)
Provided concrete remediation for each gap: DNS with short TTL, PgBouncer/HAProxy, SIGHUP handler, retry with exponential backoff, specific timeout values
Went beyond rubric requirements with valuable additions: pool exhaustion analysis, statement timeout gaps, circuit breaker recommendation, graceful degradation suggestions
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and reasoning — the 3am on-call engineer framing is effective
Minor: read replica routing described as 'unhandled' failure mode could overstate its urgency relative to the true blockers, though the verdict correctly weights it as non-blocking
